definitions.L100: No written criteria. "Good" lives in the builder's head or stakeholder opinion.
definitions.L200: Initial criteria drafted, typically cover basic capabilities, not tied to eval metrics.
definitions.L300: Written acceptance criteria covered capabilities and output quality, tied to eval metrics with thresholds. Reviewed by human experts.
definitions.L400: Criteria embedded in the release workflow — ship decisions can't proceed without referencing them. Used consistently across versions.
definitions.L500: Criteria evolve based on production signals — user complaints, new failure modes, shifting context feed back into updates.

definitions.L100: No established eval set. Quality checks are informal — demos, spot-checks, occasional manual review.
definitions.L200: A handful of test cases, written once based on key scenarios and what came to mind. Used occasionally.
definitions.L300: Versioned eval set, coverage purposefully targeted at what matters — quality dimensions, edge cases, failure modes. Mapped to risk and value.
definitions.L400: Eval set maintained as an artifact — new cases added when production issues surface, scope expands, or new failure modes emerge.
definitions.L500: Eval set's coverage itself is measured — gaps hunted, production data mined for missing cases, false pass/fail rates tracked.

definitions.L100: No routine evals. Quality is assessed through demos or occasional manual checks when something seems off.
definitions.L200: Evals run on changes. Results are logged.
definitions.L300: Offline evals run at defined trigger points (pre-deploy, post-change, regular cadence). Production quality tracked across multiple eval dimensions on a defined cadence.
definitions.L400: Evals embedded in deployment workflow — triggered by change events. Results block deploys or surface clear signals for review.
definitions.L500: Offline and production eval form a single closed loop. Production traces continuously sample into the offline eval set; offline regressions trigger production investigation.

pillars.name: Improve and iterate
pillars.mechanism: Stage 4 (Interpret) — only if eval results are available

definitions.L100: Improvements are reactive — driven by noticed issues. Fixes are intuition-based; nothing confirms they worked or whether they introduced new problems.
definitions.L200: Failing cases are logged, triaged, and prioritized. Fixes tracked to closure.
definitions.L300: Failure categories analyzed for root cause. Fixes validated with before/after eval runs. Regression-proofing built in.
definitions.L400: Improvement prioritization is eval-driven — weakest dimensions addressed first. Fix velocity and fix quality both tracked.
definitions.L500: Improvements prioritized, validated, and measured entirely through eval evidence. When alternatives exist, better scores win. Gut feel no longer drives decisions.

definitions.L100: Changes ship on spot-checks and intuition. Regressions surface via user complaints. All changes — prompts, tools, models, architecture — feel risky. Team delays or pushes through blindly.
definitions.L200: Before any change ships, eval set is rerun and compared to the prior baseline. Evidence, not intuition, decides whether to proceed.
definitions.L300: Each change type — prompt tweaks, tool swaps, model upgrades, architecture changes — triggers the right eval subset. Baseline comparison required — both to validate improvements and to catch regressions.
definitions.L400: Change workflows are eval-driven: proposed changes trigger the right eval protocol, and evidence decides — block regressions, accept improvements. No change ships unvalidated; good changes aren't held back by caution.
definitions.L500: Eval is used not just to validate changes, but to actively scan for them. Team runs new explorations through the eval set to find improvement opportunities.

items: Eval plan with acceptance criteria and Value × Cost matrix (.docx)
Test case CSVs importable into Copilot Studio (.csv per quality signal)
eval-setup-guide-<agent>-<date>.docx — step-by-step Copilot Studio setup: per-method configuration, threshold guidance, troubleshooting
rerun-protocol-<agent>-<date>.docx — when to re-run evals as the agent changes (Pillar 3 L200)
baseline-comparison-<agent>-<date>.xlsx — workbook for comparing two eval runs (Pillar 5 L200)
Triage report with root-cause analysis — if eval results are available
